source_jobs.title: Advanced Medical Assistant - MDPCP
source_jobs.description: <p>&nbsp;</p><p><strong>Position Objective:</strong></p><p><strong>Advanced Medical Assistant </strong></p><p>The Advanced Medical Assistant is an essential part of the Advanced Primary Care Team and facilitates meeting the goals of the Maryland Primary Care Program and Value Based Contracts.&nbsp; Their main responsibilities are to coordinate patient care, provide patient outreach and education and identify barriers that may affect the patient from meeting his/her treatment goals. The Advanced Medical Assistant will perform a variety of administrative and clinical tasks to support the provider/practice to ensure the patient receives the right care at the right time and right place to decrease hospital re-admission and unnecessary utilization of Emergency Room and/or Urgent Care. The Advanced Medical Assistant maintains knowledge of care coordination/management, behavioral health resources, CRISP Portal/Reports, Value Based Contract Portals, EPIC Registries and patient self-management tools/resources.<br> <strong>Essential Job Duties:</strong></p><ol><li>Monitor and validate patient attribution to the practice and the care teams within the practice (known as empanelment).</li><li>Ensure patients identified as increased risk receive targeted, proactive, relationship-based (longitudinal) care coordination and/or care management.</li><li>Pre-visit planning workflow for Annual Wellness Visits scheduled with the LPN Team and/or practice provider.</li><li>Provide patient education and resources to engage the patient in his/her treatment plan and improve their overall health.</li><li>Actively&nbsp;manages assigned panel of to develop relationship with patient as an integral member of team, provide follow-up contact with patient as indicated to ensure compliance with recommendations medications, lab/x-ray, specialist visits, PCP visits, dieticians, CDE, etc.</li><li>Maintains access to all Value Based Payor portals for attestation of metrics and/or to pull appropriate care gap and utilization reports.</li><li>Provides patient outreach for TCM/ED visits within required timeframe, i.e. within 48 hours inpatient discharge; within 7 days emergency room discharge.&nbsp; The Advanced Medical Assistant will schedule a follow up appointment with the patients primary care provider or Advanced Internal Medicine.</li><li>Assist patients in setting SMART goals for self -management, teaching them how to do self-management tasks and report abnormal findings to their provider team;</li><li>Assess barriers when patient has not met treatments goals, is not following treatment plan of care, or has not kept important appointments; refers patient to the Community Care Management Team (RN, LCSW, CHW) for intervention.</li><li>Identify patient social and behavioral health needs, consult with clinician, as appropriate provide warm hand off to Ambulatory Care Management, social work, care navigation, and behavioral health services.</li><li>Collaborate with providers, and other frequently used healthcare resources to develop effective timely communication between the patients care teams; and in assessing the patients progress toward individual health care goals</li><li>Oversees the development, procurement, and adoption of patient self-management educational resources used by the primary clinical teams.</li><li>Ensure targeted patients who have received follow-up after ED, hospital discharge, or other triggering events receive short-term (episodic) care management if applicable.</li><li>Contribute to a positive experience for patients and families through courteous telephone interactions and interview activities, accurate and expeditious routing, as well as referral to appropriate clinical

source_jobs.title: Advanced Practice Provider (APP) - GI
source_jobs.description: <p><strong>Luminis Health</strong></p><p>Title: Advanced Practice Provider (APP)- GI</p><p>Department: POB Suite GI</p><p>Reports To: Chair – Medicine</p><p>Cost Center/Job Code: 30001-40104-002075</p><p>FLSA Status: Exempt</p><p><strong>Position Objective:</strong></p><p>Diagnose and treat problems in the digestive system through direct care, consultation, research, expert guidance, leadership, ethical decision making, and collaboration. The position is to augment a developing GI program with anticipation for approximately 50% in-patient and 50% out-patient clinical activity. Excellent interpersonal and communications skills are sought with an empathetic approach, and collegiality with allied and referring providers is expected.&nbsp;&nbsp; Candidate will have specialized skills to focus on the diagnosis and treatment of issues related to the digestive tract under the supervision of a physician. As a GI nurse practitioner, your responsibilities may involve advanced assessment, diagnosis, outcomes identification, treatment/care planning, implementation, evaluation, patient education, and preparation for endoscopy procedures. Orientation to the GI specialty for suitably qualified and experienced candidates can be provided under physician guidance.</p><p><strong>Direct Patient Care</strong></p><ul><li>Provides consultations, interpretation of results and treatment plans for inpatients or outpatients as requested.</li><li>Comfort with pre-endoscopy assessment, scheduling and planning.</li><li>Care coordinating and collaboration with referral and referring colleagues.</li></ul><p><strong>Indirect Patient Care</strong></p><ul><li>Educating patients about their condition and treatment options</li><li>Providing information to patients about diet changes and lifestyle modifications that can improve their health</li><li>Commitment to lifelong learning and participation in department educational and research activities.</li></ul><p><strong>Educational/Experience Requirements:</strong></p><ul><li>Physician Assistant Degree or Nurse Practitioner</li><li>Orthopedic experience</li></ul><p><strong>Required License/Certifications:</strong></p><ul><li>Licensed as a Physician’s Assistant or Nurse Practitioner in the State of Maryland or eligible</li></ul><p><strong>Required Minimum Experience/Qualifications</strong>: At least 3 years of clinical experience.</p><p><strong>Preferred Qualifications</strong>: Teaching experience. Experience in program development. source_jobs.description: <p><strong>Position Objective:</strong></p><p>The Anestheisa Technologist will report to the Anesthesia Team Coordinator and Team Lead under the supervision of the unit Clinical Director.</p><p>The Anesthesia Technologist will independently maintain and organize the anesthesia work space, equipment, and supplies.</p><p>The AT, under supervision, will assist the Anesthesia provider, in multiple hospital locations, in preparation and performance of a variety of anesthesia functions.</p><p><strong>Essential Job Duties:&nbsp;<br></strong>Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.&nbsp;</p><p>1.)&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; The Anesthesia Technologist will prepare the anesthesia machines for use by performing daily quality checks and ensuring optimal anesthesia machine function.</p><p>2.)&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Demonstrates detailed technical knowledge of anesthesia equipment systems for troubleshooting and maintenance.</p><p>3.)&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Sets up anesthesia machine and maintains all anesthesia equipment, instruments and supplies, before, during and after the procedure, according to par levels.</p><p>4.)&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Prepares for induction of anesthesia by placing non-invasive monitors (BP cuff, ECG leads and Pulse Oximeter) at request of provider and is prepared to provide support and anticipate needs during induction and emergence of anesthesia.</p><p>5.)&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Assists in sterile and aseptic preparation for placement of invasive lines or devices, under supervision. After placement of invasive lines or devices, cleans and disposes of used items in appropriate trash receptacle, or prepares items for processing following standard operating room procedure.</p><p>6.)&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Demonstrates knowledge of aseptic technique by preparing and maintaining sterility during invasive procedures.</p><p>7.)&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Anticipates needs for invasive monitoring by having appropriate equipment in operating room prior to procedure, or upon request of anesthesia provider.</p><p>8.)&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Differentiates and anticipates the supplies and equipment needed during emergent situations and for specific patient populations (peds, geriatric, etc.) including but not limited to difficult airway, cardiac arrest, fire, malignant hyperthermia and blood administration.</p><p>9.)&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Maintains knowledge and follows patient and personal safety procedures policies and national standards related to infection control, fire safety, electrical or chemical hazards.</p><p>10.)The Anesthesia Technologist will work to ensure the delivery of safe patient care within &nbsp;their scope of practice and participate in a positive and professional work environment.&nbsp;</p><p><strong>Education/Experience Requirements:</strong></p><p><strong>Required Minimum Education</strong></p><ul><li>Associates degree or equivalent</li><li>Successful completion of accredited Anesthesia Technician Program.</li><li>2 years experience as Anesthesia Technologist</li></ul><p><strong>Preferred Experience</strong></p><ul><li>Cardiac OR experience highly preferred&nbsp;</li></ul><p><strong>Required License/Certifications:</strong></p><ul><li>American Heart Association Healthcare Provider BLS</li><li>ACLS required within 6 months of hire.</li><li>Certification by the American Association of Anesthesia Technicians and Technologists (ASATT)</li></u

source_jobs.title: APP - Inpatient Ortho
source_jobs.description: <p><strong>Position Objective:</strong>&nbsp;</p><p>The APP will be responsible for the evaluation and &nbsp;&nbsp;management of patients within the emergency department and within the hospital.</p><p><strong>Essential Job Duties:</strong></p><ol><li>Performs comprehensive and problem-focused histories and physical examinations</li><li>Interprets laboratory and diagnostic tests and correlates subjective and objective data to formulate a differential diagnosis and establish a working diagnosis</li><li>Formulates a treatment plan for orthopedic conditions within their scope of practice and implements therapeutic intervention for specific conditions when appropriate</li><li>Exercises judgment on conditions requiring consultation, referral or evaluation by the supervising orthopedic surgeon or other health care professionals</li><li>Orders, collects and performs appropriate laboratory and diagnostic studies, such as routine laboratory tests, EMG, electrocardiogram and radiologic examinations including arthrogram, ultrasound, CT, MRI and bone scan studies</li><li>Performs minor outpatient surgical procedures such as tendon repair, wound closure and management, incision and drainage, needle biopsy percutaneous pinning of fractures k-wire and hardware removal, compartment pressure measurements, digital blocks and regional anesthesia</li><li>Performs diagnostic and therapeutic procedures such as aspirations and injections of joints, bursars and cysts as well as closed reductions of fractures and dislocations</li><li>Applies braces, casts, splints and other orthopedic appliances</li><li>Assists in the surgical suite including first assist, deep and superficial wound closure, application of internal devices and any other procedures delegated by the supervising orthopedic surgeon</li><li>Conducts hospital rounds, writes orders, takes call and sees patients in the ED for the orthopedic surgeon and formulates and implements treatment and therapeutic plans for hospitalized</li></ol><p><strong>Educational/Experience Requirements:</strong></p><ul><li>Physician Assistant Degree or Nurse Practitioner</li><li>Orthopedic experience</li></ul><p><strong>Required License/Certifications:</strong></p><ul><li>Licensed as a Physician’s Assistant or Nurse Practitioner in the State of Maryland source_jobs.title: Cardiac Surgery Quality Coordinator (RN)
source_jobs.description: <p class="MsoNormal"><strong>Position Objective:</strong></p><p class="MsoNormal">The Cardiac Surgery Quality Coordinator is a critical role dedicated to advancing patient outcomes and operational excellence within the adult cardiac surgery program. This position is responsible for the comprehensive review and management of all Adult Cardiac Society of Thoracic Surgeons (STS) quality metrics. The Coordinator serves as the primary liaison between external data abstractors and internal clinical teams, seeking to educate partners on areas of operational excellence, areas for process improvement, and any areas where quality improvement are needed. Leveraging strong clinical expertise, the Coordinator will collaborate with surgical, anesthesia, and nursing partners to identify, develop, and lead targeted quality improvement initiatives.</p><p class="MsoNormal"><strong>Essential Job Duties:</strong></p><p class="MsoNormal"><strong>Quality Improvement, Education &amp; Clinical Collaboration</strong></p><p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpFirst"><strong>1.<span style="font: 7.0pt 'Times New Roman'">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; </span></strong>Clinical Liaison: Act as the primary bridge between the external data abstraction team and internal clinical staff, ensuring seamless communication and mutual understanding of quality goals.</p><p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle"><strong>2.<span style="font: 7.0pt 'Times New Roman'">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; </span></strong>Clinical Education &amp; Feedback: Proactively educate multidisciplinary partners (surgeons, anesthesia, nursing) on STS metrics, highlighting current areas of operational excellence, identifying specific opportunities for process and quality improvement, and leading quality improvement data conferences.</p><p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle"><strong>3.<span style="font: 7.0pt 'Times New Roman'">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; </span></strong>Process Improvement: Design, implement, and manage key quality improvement (QI) projects based on STS data findings to enhance patient safety, streamline clinical workflows, and improve post-operative outcomes.</p><p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle"><strong>4.<span style="font: 7.0pt 'Times New Roman'">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; </span></strong>Protocol Development: Collaborate with clinical leadership to update or establish evidence-based care protocols, standardizing pathways for cardiac surgery patients based on identified areas of need.</p><p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle"><strong>5.<span style="font: 7.0pt 'Times New Roman'">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; </span></strong>External Liaison: Represents Luminis Health in Maryland Cardiac Surgery Quality Initiative (MCSQI) conferences, data workshops and/or research efforts, and participates in state-level Cardiac Surgery Quality forums as appropriate.</p><p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpLast"><strong>6.<span style="font: 7.0pt 'Times New Roman'">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; source_jobs.title: Case Manager - PT - Days @LHAAMC
source_jobs.description: <p><strong>Position Objective:</strong>&nbsp;&nbsp;</p><p>The&nbsp;Case Manager&nbsp;works under the direction of the clinical director of care management, providing coordination of care for patients at Luminis Health to support safe, seamless, timely transitions across the continuum.&nbsp; Utilizing a collaborative process, will identify (using quantitative and qualitative methods), assess, plan, implement and evaluate the options and services required to meet an individual’s health and health related needs, including social- determinants that affect ones’ overall wellbeing.&nbsp;Promotes the right resources, at the right time and at the right level of care and is responsible for engaging and supporting patients that are in need of care management services; is able to determine, using evidence based guidelines, the correct initial and ongoing level of care for patients and is able to submit appropriate denial review for Medicare, Medicaid and commercial insurers.</p><p><strong>Essential Job Duties:</strong></p><p>&nbsp;Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.</p><p>&nbsp;1. Identifies and prioritizes patient in need of care management services, using a holistic approach inclusive of biopsychosocial, functional, cultural, spiritual, and financial factors; uses a multi discoplinary approach to assess/plan for care needs.</p><p>2. Identifies and implements strategies such as motivational interviewing to promote patient engagement, self-care, treatment adherence, and optimal levels of health and well-being.</p><p>3. &nbsp;Utilizes evidenced based guidelines (such as InterQual or other agreed upon evidenced based guidelines) to promote quality care, decrease variation and mitigate waste. Verifies appropriate level of care; enters clinical review and authorized days&nbsp;in Epic; documents actions to avoid denied days; refers cases to Physcian Advisor as appropriate.</p><p>4. Manages observation stay patients assertively and ensures timely testing, &nbsp;treatment and conversion to inpatient status or discharge.&nbsp;&nbsp;</p><p>5. Develops and coordinates transition plans for patients transitioned to home with home health, community care coordination program, Hospice or Palliative care, home infusion and routine sub-acute and skilled post-acute providers; completes all necessary documentation and necessary handovers. &nbsp;Involves and prepares patients and families for transition from the ED, Peds, Clatanoff&nbsp;or Observation unit as indicated.&nbsp;</p><p>6. Maintains clear and concise documentation in each patient record to reflect physical and functional limitations, psychosocial characteristics, educational needs of patient &amp; family, family/social support systems, financial, economic, and transition needs. Initiates referrals to disciplines as indicated.</p><p>7. Participates in nursing unit and department clinical outcome projects as well as process improvement initiatives of care management.&nbsp;</p><p>8. Identifies potential or current patient situations which require referral to other members of the health care team such as infection control, risk management, or quality management.&nbsp; Assures plan of care is adjusted as appropriate and that follow-up occurs.&nbsp; Keep leadership abreast of potential issues.</p><p>9. Utilizes all risk and predictive analytic tools such as the readmission risk tool.&nbsp; Applies tailored interventions to mitigate potential barriers or risk, prolonged unnecessary hospitalization and readmission prevention.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</p><p>10. Maintains compliance with all regulatory standards (CMS, commercial insurers etc)</p><p&g

source_jobs.description: <p><strong>Position Objective:</strong></p><p>Under the supervision of the Cash Operations Manager, the Cash Operations Representative is responsible for the validation and reconciliation of money and processing of all payments, adjustments, denials accurately and timely for all professional fee billing. &nbsp;Determines if applied payments should be refunded; monitors credit work queues and resolves credit balances stemming from over posted adjustments, payments. &nbsp;&nbsp;Researches patient’s accounts on all requested refunds and completes necessary paperwork and process to finalize refunds. <br> <strong>Essential Job Duties:</strong></p><p>The Cash Operations Representative is responsible for bank reconciliation and accurate posting of payments , adjustments, denials , recoupments and refunds while adhering to compliance, policies and regulations.</p><p><strong>Responsibilities:</strong></p><ol><li>Records monies received in multiple bank accounts and lock boxes daily and reconcile the posting of money to current and legacy AR systems on a daily and monthly basis accurately.</li><li>Receives and records all electronic and non-electronic payments from lockbox or other locations daily.</li><li>Identifies system payments and works with Cash Operations Lead/Manager to communicate funds received. Prepares and submits journal entries or money reclassifications for accuracy daily to Cash Operations Lead/Manager.</li><li>Reconciles all payment, adjustment and refund batches to the daily journal. Resolves any out of balance batches before closing. Responsible for reporting to Cash Operation Lead/Manager any problems or issues on the monthly reconciliation.</li><li>Validates receipt and loading of electronic remittance files, resolves errors according to policy and procedure and accepts files to post payments electronically daily.</li><li>Post, reviews, scans and disburses self-pay and insurance lock box documents to appropriate resources/ external teams/units timely.</li><li>Maintains manual posting batches chronologically by date of entry to the system to facilitate quick and easy access.</li><li>Posts all manual payments, adjustments, denials and recoupments manually and electronically to the correct patient account/ invoice.&nbsp;</li><li>Transfers secondary balances to the correct payer or patient as the account and payers’ explanations of benefits dictates. Forwards copies of rejections or incorrect payments to the Accounts Receivable Specialist for research and resolution.</li><li>Performs other duties as assigned.</li></ol><p><strong>Educational/Experience Requirements:</strong></p><ul><li>High School Diploma or equivalent (GED) required.</li><li>Excellent intrapersonal and communication skills required to and the ability to work collaboratively in a team setting as well as external vendors /departments and units.</li><li>Two or more years of experience performing the posting of self- pay and insurance payments, adjustments, denials and correspondence manually and electronically preferred.</li><li>Multi-division cash application processing experience with knowledge of lockbox manual and electronic file processing and banking reconciliation for multiple bank accounts.&nbsp;</li><li>Highly organized &nbsp;and flexible with the ability to re prioritize work&nbsp; and accept new&nbsp; or different assignments daily as needed to ensure completion of work..</li></ul><p><strong>Preferred Qualifications:</strong></p><ul><li>EPIC Cash Management Tool and payment posting module experience preferred</li><li>Experience working with lock box conv

source_jobs.description: <p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal"><strong><span style="font-size: 12.0pt; font-family: 'Times New Roman',serif;">Position Objective:</span></strong></p><p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal"><span style="font-size: 12.0pt; font-family: 'Times New Roman',serif;">At Luminis Diabetes Center, we are dedicated to improving the lives of individuals living with diabetes through comprehensive education, personalized care, and innovative treatment approaches. Our mission is to empower patients with the knowledge and tools necessary to manage their condition effectively and achieve optimal health outcomes. We value compassion, collaboration, and excellence in care delivery as we strive to be a leader in diabetes education and management. The educator will be responsible for individual and group teaching, regulatory and quality reporting requirements, and the coordination of other Diabetes Center activities and services. The educator works in a collaborative management structure with our Executive Medical Director, the Lead Certified Diabetes Care and Education Specialist (CDCES), the inpatient Certified Diabetes Care Education Specialist Team, and the Administrative Team.</span></p><p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal"><strong><span style="font-size: 12.0pt; font-family: 'Times New Roman',serif;">Position Summary:</span></strong></p><p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal"><span style="font-size: 12.0pt; font-family: 'Times New Roman',serif;">We are seeking a highly motivated and experienced Certified Diabetes Care Education Specialist (CDCES) to join our interdisciplinary team. This role demands a passionate (CDCES) committed to promoting self-management skills, improving patient outcomes, and supporting the overall mission of Luminis Diabetes Center.</span></p><p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal"><strong><span style="font-size: 12.0pt; font-family: 'Times New Roman',serif;">Essential Job Duties:</span></strong></p><p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal"><span style="font-size: 12.0pt; font-family: 'Times New Roman',serif;">Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions</span></p><p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal"><span style="font-size: 12.0pt; font-family: 'Times New Roman',serif;">1) Conduct comprehensive diabetes self-management education (DSME) sessions for individuals and groups, tailored to patients’ clinical needs and learning styles.&nbsp; </span></p><p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal"><span style="font-size: 12.0pt; font-family: 'Times New Roman',serif;">2) Develop personalized care plans in collaboration with physicians, dietitians, nurses, and other healthcare professionals to optimize diabetes management.&nbsp; </span></p><p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal"><span style="font-size: 12.0pt; font-family: 'Times New Roman',serif;">3) Provide ongoing assessment and support to patients regarding medication adherence, lifestyle modifications, glucose monitoring, and complication prevention.&nbsp; </span></p><p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: normal&q
